title: Homemade Tn5 transposome assembly and activity evaluation
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3o5ca","text":"This protocol is suitable for the assembly of purified Tn5 protein with a concentration between 0.5~1 μg/μl.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7ncki","text":"This protocol is basically for homemade Tn5 assembly and activity evaluation in ISSAAC-seq. Tn5 activity is determined by bulk ATAC-seq experiments, which mimics the in situ tagmentation condition in ISSAAC-seq experiments.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":166,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e2i54","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ci3h6","text":"Unloaded Tn5 is available from vendors such as Diagenode (C01070010-10) or Lucigen (Cat. No. TNP92110). We obtain Tn5 from a local vendor, supplied at approximately 0.5 μg/μl.  Also, there are many standard protocols for Tn5 purification, and you can produce your own Tn5 protein if needed. Here are some references for Tn5 purification:","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6sa5e","text":"1) Generation and Purification of pTXB1.Tn5 (protocols.io)","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":3,"length":55}],"data":{}},{"key":"8bl9d","text":"2) Picelli, Simone, et al. \"Tn5 transposase and tagmentation procedures for massively scaled sequencing projects.\" Genome research 24.12 (2014): 2033-2040.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7hrht","text":"3) Soroczynski,J., et al., OpenTn5 Project: Open-source resource for robust and scalable Tn5 transposase purification and characterization. Molecular Biology of the Cell, 2023. 34(2): p. 1026-1026.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"03267366C6D411ECB7380A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/generation-and-purification-of-ptxb1-tn5-eq2lydoyrlx9/v1"}}}}

steps: Mix 25ul ME_A (100μM) with 25ul ME_Bottom (100μM) in a PCR tube → 50ul Annealed ME_A (50μM) Mix 25ul ME_B (100μM) with 25ul ME_Bottom (100μM) in a PCR tube → 50ul Annealed ME_B (50μM)

Annealing: by denaturing the oligos at 98oC first, and then slowly lowering the temperature from 98oC to 25oC, ME_A or ME_B will gradually anneal with ME_Bottom. Put the oligo mixtures into a PCR thermocycler, run the following program: TemperatureTimeNumber of cycles 98oC5min1 98oCDecrease the temperature by 0.5oC at every 30 seconds 50 73oCDecrease the temperature by 0.5oC at every 30 seconds 96 16oC∞1 (Alternatively, you can incubate the tubes in ~2 liters of boiling water, and let the ~98oC water slowly cool down to room temperature; or incubate them in a 98oC heating block and turn off the heating block to allow it to slowly cool down to room temperature.)
Mix 50 ul of 50 μM annealed ME_A and 50 ul of 50 μM annealed ME_B → 100 ul of annealed ME_A + ME_B (50 μM total; 25 μM each)
20 ul of annealed ME_A + ME_B (50 μM total; 25 μM each) + 30 ul H2O → 50 ul of annealed ME_A + ME_B (20 μM total; 10 μM each)

Assemble the Tn5 enzyme and annealed oligos: ComponentAmount Tn5 (L)~48 ul Annealed ME_A + ME_B (20 μM total; 10 μM each) 24 ul Coupling Buffer88 ul Total~160 ul ComponentAmount Tn5 (H)~48 ul Annealed ME_A + ME_B (20 μM total; 10 μM each) 24 ul Coupling Buffer88 ul Total~160 ul
Reagent Setup

Prepare Coupling Buffer: %s %s Component Stock concentration Concentration For 1 ml Tris-HCl, pH=7.5 1 M 50 mM 50 μl NaCl 5 M 100 mM 20 μl EDTA, pH=8.0 0.5 M 0.1 mM 0.2 μl Triton X-100 10% 0.1% 10 μl DTT 1 M 1 mM 1 μl Glycerol 100% 50% 500 μl H2O - - 412 μl Store at -20 °C for up to 6 months.
Adapter annealing.
Mix 25 µL ME_S5 oligo (100 μM) with 25 µL ME_Bottom oligo (100 μM) in a PCR tube, and label as "S5_adapter".

Prepare oligonucleotides： %s %s #name seq (5'-3') ME_S5 TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG ME_S7 GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG ME_bottom Phos-C*T*G*T*C*T*C*T*T*A*T*A*C*A*C*A*T*C*/iInvdT/ Dissolve the oligos in annealing buffer from above, to a final concentration of 100 μM. Store at -20 °C for up to 6 months.

Assemble the Tn5 enzyme and annealed oligos: A B 1 Component Amount (μl) 2 Tn5 48 3 Annealed S5_adapter (20 μM） 12 4 Annealed S7_adapter (20 μM) 12 5 Coupling buffer 69 6 Total 141 Incubate at room temperature for 01:00:00 . Store at -20 °C for up to 1 year.
Mix 25 µL ME_S7 oligo (100 μM) with 25 µL ME_Bottom oligo (100 μM) in a PCR tube, and label as "S7_adapter".
Dilute the S5_adaptor ( 50 micromolar (µM) ) and S7_adaptor( 50 micromolar (µM) ) with nuclease-free water to( 20 micromolar (µM) ). Continue to Step 8 or store at -20 °C for up to 6 months.
Anneal adaptor oligo mixtures in a Thermocycler with the following reaction: 98 °C 00:03:00 slowly cooled to 16 °C with a temperature ramp of −0.1 °C/s. 16 °C hold.

The concentration and purity of in-house purified Tn5 protein could be further confirmed by Western blot before you start assembly. Tn5 protein is about 53 kDa, and its target band is between 55 kDa and 70 kDa markers on the gel.
Prepare BSA protein concentration standards: dilute 3 µL of 2 mg/ml BSA standard with 21 µL of water to a final concentration of 0.25 mg/ml.

Prepare BSA standards for WB： A B C D E 1 Standard No. 0.25 mg/ml BSA (μl) Water (μl) 5x loading (μl) Amount (μg） 2 1 1 15 4 0.25 3 2 2 14 4 0.50 4 3 4 12 4 1.00 5 4 6 10 4 1.50 6 5 8 8 4 2.00 Incubate at 95 °C for 00:05:00 , centrifuge briefly rpm, °C, /.
For each batch, mix 2 µL Tn5, 4 µL 5x loading buffer, and 14 µL water. Incubate at 95 °C for 00:05:00 , centrifuge briefly rpm, °C, /.

Load the samples on a SDS-PAGE gel. After electrophoresis, the protein was transferred to NC membrane and stained with Ponceau S. Compare Tn5 with BSA standards to determine protein concentration. Expected result 1 Tn5 concentration detremination by WB. ( Sample T1-T4 are four different batches of purified Tn5 protein.The concentrations of samples T1 to T4 are all between 0.5-0.75 μg/μl, and can be used for the next step of assembly. Sample T4 has a relatively high concentration.Therefore, assembled T4-Tn5 may require a reduced amount to achieve similar cleavage efficiency compared to other groups.) Expected result 2 Tn5 concentration detremination by WB. (1024 and 0617 are two different batches of purified Tn5. The concentration of batch1024 is about 0.5 μg/μl, and can be used for the next step of assembly. Batch 0607's concentration is about 0.25 μg/μl, which is not suitable for this protocol and needs to be further concentrated.)
Perform a fast ATAC-seq experiment to determine the activity of assembled Tn5.

Prepare 4x THS TD Buffer: A B C 1 Reagent Final concentration For 1 ml 2 1 M Tris-HCl (pH 8.0) 132 mM 132 μl 3 5 M potassium acetate 264 mM 52.8 µl 4 1 M Magnesium acetate 40 mM 40 µl 5 N,N-dimethylformamide 64% 640 µl 6 Nuclease-free water - 135.2 µl

Prepare 0.1% (wt/vol) digitonin: Mix 0.5 µL 2% (wt/vol) digitonin stock with 9.5 µL nuclease-free water. CRITICAL Make fresh. The Promega 2% (wt/vol) digitonin stock is dissolved in DMSO. Thaw at RT prior to use.
Pellet 50,000 cells for each sample (#1 illumina Tn5，#2 Tn5_batch1, #3 Tn5_batch2…）at 1,000g, 4 °C for 3 min in a fixed-angle centrifuge.
Aspirate and discard all supernatants, resuspend cell pellets in 1 mL ice-cold 1× DPBS–0.5% (wt/vol) BSA and centrifuge at 1,000g, 4 °C for 3 min.

Aspirate all supernatants using a P1000 pipette, and briefly centrifuge again to collect leftover buffer from the bottom of the tube. Remove traces of the buffer using a P200 pipette with a 10 μL tip fitted on top of a 200 μL tip.

Prepare 50 µL tagmentation mix for each sample by combining and mixing the reagents listed in the table below: A B C 1 Reagent Amount (µL) Final concentration 2 4× THS TD buffer 12.5 1× 3 0.1% (wt/vol) Digitonin 5 0.01% (wt/vol) 4 Tn5 2.5 - 5 Nuclease-free water 30 - 6 Total 50 -
Resuspend the cell pellet in 50 µL tagmentation mix by pipetting up and down 25 times.
Put the 50 µL reaction on a thermomixer, and incubate at 37 °C, 800 rpm for 30 min.
Cleanup reaction with a Zymo DNA Clean and Concentrator-5 kit, elute DNA in 21 μL of elution buffer.
Set up the following PCR reaction: A B 1 Reagent Amount (µL) 2 Purified sample 8 3 N7 primer (10 µM) 1 4 S5 primer (10 µM) 1 5 NEBNext High-Fidelity 2× PCR Master Mix 10 6 Total 20
Take out 9 µL of each reaction, and mix with 1 µL 10X SYBR Green and perform a qPCR analysis to decide the cycle number.

Use the following cycling condition to perform a qPCR analysis, and monitor the amplification curve in linear scale. A B C D 1 Steps Temperature(℃) Time Cycles 2 Gap fill-in 72 5 min 1 3 Initial Denaturation 98 1 min 1 4 Denaturation 98 10 s 30 5 Annealing 63 30 s 6 Extension 72 20 s Amplification Plot of qPCR. The Ct values of Tn5_lot1024 are comparable to illumina_Tn5, thus can be used in subsequent experiments. Amplification Plot of qPCR. The Ct value of lot0607 was significantly different from that of the illumina control, indicating that the purification may fail, and this batch of Tn5 cannot be used in subsequent experiments.

Prepare Annealing Buffer: %s %s Component Stock concentration Final concentration For 1 ml Tris-HCl, pH=8.0 1 M 10 mM 10 μl NaCl 5 M 50 mM 10 μl EDTA, pH=8.0 0.5 M 1 mM 2 μl H2O -- -- 978 μl Store at -20 °C for up to 6 months.

Validation of ATAC-seq library fragment length distribution. Amplify the rest 11 μL PCR reaction for 12 cycles, purify the PCR product using 1.2x VAHTS DNA Clean Beads, elute in 20 μL water and check the size distribution using a bioanalyzer. Distribution of ATAC-seq libraries. (Successful experiments with a prominent nucleosomal ladder pattern.)
creator: Wei Xu
